_**User (2026-05-04 12:01:02Z)**_

V2 Schema

**Runs Table**

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| id | UUID | No | gen\_random\_uuid() | Primary Key; **Generated by the database.** |
| user\_id | TEXT | No | — | Referenced from PRISM |
| started\_at | TIMESTAMP | No | — |  |
| finished\_at | TIMESTAMP | No | — |  |
| expires\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | upload time \+ 365 days |  |
| pinned | BOOLEAN | No | FALSE |  |
| system\_info | JSONB | No | — |  |
| config | JSONB | No | — | Converted from YAML to JSONB |
| result\_summary | JSONB | No | — |  |
| archive\_uri | TEXT | No |  | Some place in BLOB where we can access the whole submission archive. |

**Indexes:** user\_id·, id.

**API Needs:**  
**\-** CREATE a run, by sending all data to the database.

- The CLI will handle uploading to BLOB, and then send the data to the database

\- LIST runs for a particular user / submitter.  
**\-** We’ll probably want the default behavior to be the submitter, not the user, and then filter to just the user runs. I imagine other users will want to check out the runs.  
\- VIEW run data for a particular run id. CLI will download from blob storage, but API will return all data in storage with an optarg.  
\- PIN a run, gated by submitter group / user id.  
\- UNPIN a run, gated by submitter group / user id.  
\- DELETE a run, gated by submitter group / user id.

**Submissions Table**

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| id | UUID | No | gen\_random\_uuid() | Primary Key |
| user\_id | TEXT | No | — |  |
| created\_at | TIMESTAMP | No | NOW() |  |
| status | TEXT | No | 'COMPLIANCE\_CHECKING' | See valid\_status constraint |
| benchmark\_version | INT | No | 1 |  |
| division | TEXT | No | — | standardized | serviced | rdi |
| availability | TEXT | No | — | available | preview | rdi |
| early\_publish | BOOLEAN | No | FALSE |  |
| publication\_cycle | TEXT | Yes | NULL | YYYY-MM-CN |
| target\_availability\_date | DATE | Yes | NULL | Required if preview |
| availability\_qualified\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL |  |
| compliance\_passed\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle Timestamps |
| first\_published\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle Timestamps |
| peer\_review\_started\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle Timestamps |
| objection\_resolution\_started\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle Timestamps |
| finalized\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle Timestamps |
| withdrawn\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle Timestamps |
| run\_ids | UUID\[\] | No | — |  |
| archive\_uri | TEXT | Yes | NULL | Pointer to blob storage |
|  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |
| pr\_url | TEXT | Yes | NULL |  |
| pr\_number | INT | Yes | NULL |  |

**API Needs:**

- CREATE **SUBMISSION**  
- LIST SUBMISSIONS for a particular ID.  
- View all data for a submission  
  - **This will download all data for all pertinent runs in a submission, and is akin to running the download command for each run separately.**  
- WITHDRAW submissions.  
  - Sets withdrawn\_at to NOW(), and changes status accordingly.  
- Update timestamps, and PR status.  
  - Availability qualification, and all other data.  
  - May want to restrict this to certain users / systems.

**Submitters / Submission Groups Table**

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| id | UUID | NO | get\_random\_uuid() | Primary Key |
| user\_id | UUID | NO | “FK” to user\_id in PRISM | User ID in PRISM |
| organization | TEXT | NO | user organization | Used in peer review process |

V1 Schema   
Note: I’m just focusing on the runs and submissions as the archive can be handled with some minimal SQL processing and the peer-reviews should be in GitHub for the first stage, at least until we get to the point of hosting our own reviews ( if we want such functionality ).

**Runs Table**

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| id | UUID | No | gen\_random\_uuid() | PK |
| user\_id | TEXT | No | — |  |
| started\_at | TIMESTAMP | No | — |  |
| finished\_at | TIMESTAMP | No | — |  |
| expires\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | NULL \= pinned |
| pinned | BOOLEAN | No | FALSE |  |
| system\_info | JSONB | No | — |  |
| config | JSONB | No | — |  |
| result\_summary | JSONB | No | — |  |

**Indexes:** idx\_runs\_user ON (user\_id) · idx\_runs\_expires ON (expires\_at) WHERE expires\_at IS NOT NULL

**Constraint:** pinned\_implies\_no\_expiry — NOT pinned OR expires\_at IS NULL

---

**Submissions Table**

| Column | Type | Nullable | Default | Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| id | UUID | No | gen\_random\_uuid() | PK |
| user\_id | TEXT | No | — |  |
| status | TEXT | No | 'COMPLIANCE\_CHECKING' | See valid\_status constraint |
| cycle | TEXT | Yes | NULL | YYYY-MM-CN |
| division | TEXT | No | — | standardized | serviced | rdi |
| availability | TEXT | No | — | available | preview | rdi |
| target\_availability\_date | DATE | Yes | NULL | Required if preview |
| availability\_qualified\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL |  |
| early\_publish | BOOLEAN | No | FALSE |  |
| run\_ids | UUID\[\] | No | — |  |
| extras | JSONB | Yes | NULL | ≤ 1 MB |
| metadata | JSONB | No | — | contact, model\_notes, etc. |
| compliance\_passed\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle clock |
| first\_published\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle clock |
| peer\_review\_started\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle clock |
| objection\_resolution\_started\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle clock |
| finalized\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle clock |
| withdrawn\_at | TIMESTAMP | Yes | NULL | Lifecycle clock |
| version | INT | No | 1 |  |
| previous\_id | UUID | Yes | NULL | FK → submissions(id) |
| pr\_url | TEXT | Yes | NULL |  |
| pr\_number | INT | Yes | NULL |  |
| created\_at | TIMESTAMP | No | NOW() |  |

**Constraint valid\_status:** COMPLIANCE\_CHECKING | PEER\_REVIEW\_PENDING | CONFIDENTIAL\_REVIEW | FINALIZED | PUBLISHED | WITHDRAWN | WITHDRAWN\_INCOMPLETE | EXPIRED | SUPERSEDED

**Constraint preview\_requires\_target:** availability \!= 'preview' OR target\_availability\_date IS NOT NULL

# MLPerf Submission API — Implementation Plan

## Context
Build a simple, working FastAPI CRUD service backed by PostgreSQL, wrapped in a devcontainer, implementing the V2 schema for **Runs**, **Submissions**, and **Submitters**. The CLI handles blob storage; this API handles database state only. Auth is header-based (`X-User-Id`) as a PRISM placeholder.

---

## File Structure

\```
/home/arav/test2/
├── .devcontainer/
│   ├── devcontainer.json          # VS Code devcontainer config
│   └── docker-compose.yml        # app (python:3.12) + db (postgres:16)
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py                   # FastAPI app, lifespan (create_all), router inclusion
│   ├── database.py               # async engine, session factory, Base
│   ├── models.py                 # SQLAlchemy ORM models (Run, Submission, Submitter)
│   ├── schemas.py                # Pydantic v2 request/response models + validators
│   ├── auth.py                   # Header-based user extraction (PRISM placeholder)
│   └── routers/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── runs.py               # Run CRUD endpoints
│       └── submissions.py        # Submission CRUD endpoints
├── migrations/
│   └── 001_init.sql              # Reference SQL (actual tables created by SQLAlchemy)
└── requirements.txt
\```

---

## Key Design Decisions

### Auth
- `X-User-Id` header required for write/mutate operations (`require_user` dependency)
- Optional for list/read operations (`optional_user` dependency)
- Ownership gating: `run.user_id == current_user` (or `submission.user_id`)

### Database
- **Driver**: `asyncpg` via `sqlalchemy[asyncio]`
- **Schema creation**: `Base.metadata.create_all` on startup (lifespan)
- **URL**: `postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mlperf` (from `DATABASE_URL` env var)

### Models & Constraints (enforced in SQLAlchemy + DB)
| Constraint | Applies to |
|---|---|
| `NOT pinned OR expires_at IS NULL` | Run |
| `status IN (...)` — 9 valid values | Submission |
| `division IN ('standardized','serviced','rdi')` | Submission |
| `availability IN ('available','preview','rdi')` | Submission |
| `availability != 'preview' OR target_availability_date IS NOT NULL` | Submission |

### `expires_at` default
On Run CREATE: if `expires_at` is null and `pinned=False`, set `expires_at = now() + 365 days`.

---

## API Endpoints

### Runs (`/runs`)
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | `/runs` | none | Create run |
| GET | `/runs?user_id=` | optional | List runs (defaults to `X-User-Id`) |
| GET | `/runs/{id}` | none | Get run by ID |
| PATCH | `/runs/{id}/pin` | required | Pin (clears expires_at), gated by owner |
| PATCH | `/runs/{id}/unpin` | required | Unpin (sets expires_at = now+365d), gated by owner |
| DELETE | `/runs/{id}` | required | Delete, gated by owner |

### Submissions (`/submissions`)
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | `/submissions` | none | Create submission |
| GET | `/submissions?user_id=` | optional | List (defaults to `X-User-Id`) |
| GET | `/submissions/{id}?include_runs=true` | none | Get submission + all run data |
| POST | `/submissions/{id}/withdraw` | required | Set withdrawn_at=now(), status=WITHDRAWN, gated by owner |
| PATCH | `/submissions/{id}` | required | Update timestamps, PR fields, status — gated by owner |

---

## Schemas (Pydantic v2)

- `RunCreate` — validates `pinned + expires_at` constraint
- `RunOut` — full run response
- `SubmissionCreate` — validates division/availability enums, preview requires target date
- `SubmissionUpdate` — optional fields: status, all lifecycle timestamps, pr_url/pr_number, archive_uri
- `SubmissionOut` — full submission response
- `SubmissionWithRuns(SubmissionOut)` — adds `runs: List[RunOut]`

---

## Devcontainer

- **app service**: `mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:3.12`, volume-mounts workspace, waits for `db` healthcheck
- **db service**: `postgres:16`, healthcheck via `pg_isready`, port 5432 exposed
- `postStartCommand`: `pip install -r /workspace/requirements.txt`
- Run server: `uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload`

---

## Verification

1. Open devcontainer in VS Code (or `docker compose up` from `.devcontainer/`)
2. Run `uvicorn app.main:app --reload` — tables created on startup
3. `GET /health` → `{"status": "ok"}`
4. `POST /runs` with JSON body → 201 + run object with UUID
5. `GET /runs?user_id=alice` → list of alice's runs
6. `PATCH /runs/{id}/pin` with `X-User-Id: alice` → pinned=true, expires_at=null
7. `POST /submissions` → 201 + submission
8. `GET /submissions/{id}` → submission with nested runs array
9. `POST /submissions/{id}/withdraw` → status=WITHDRAWN
10. FastAPI auto-docs at `http://localhost:8000/docs`

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 12:12:20Z)**_

The design is fine — FastAPI routes each combination of HTTP method + path independently, so all these coexist in one router file without conflict:

| | Path |
|---|---|
| **Create** | `POST /submissions` |
| **List** | `GET /submissions` |
| **Read** | `GET /submissions/{id}` |
| **Update** | `PATCH /submissions/{id}` |
| **Withdraw** | `POST /submissions/{id}/withdraw` |

No separate files needed per operation. FastAPI distinguishes `GET /submissions` from `GET /submissions/{id}` automatically, and `POST /submissions` from `PATCH /submissions/{id}` by method. Shall I proceed to implement?

## Approved Plan:
# MLPerf Submission API — Implementation Plan

## Context
Build a simple, working FastAPI CRUD service backed by PostgreSQL, wrapped in a devcontainer, implementing the V2 schema for **Runs**, **Submissions**, and **Submitters**. The CLI handles blob storage; this API handles database state only. Auth is header-based (`X-User-Id`) as a PRISM placeholder.

---

## File Structure

```
/home/arav/test2/
├── .devcontainer/
│   ├── devcontainer.json          # VS Code devcontainer config
│   └── docker-compose.yml        # app (python:3.12) + db (postgres:16)
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py                   # FastAPI app, lifespan (create_all), router inclusion
│   ├── database.py               # async engine, session factory, Base
│   ├── models.py                 # SQLAlchemy ORM models (Run, Submission, Submitter)
│   ├── schemas.py                # Pydantic v2 request/response models + validators
│   ├── auth.py                   # Header-based user extraction (PRISM placeholder)
│   └── routers/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── runs.py               # Run CRUD endpoints
│       └── submissions.py        # Submission CRUD endpoints
├── migrations/
│   └── 001_init.sql              # Reference SQL (actual tables created by SQLAlchemy)
└── requirements.txt
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

### Auth
- `X-User-Id` header required for write/mutate operations (`require_user` dependency)
- Optional for list/read operations (`optional_user` dependency)
- Ownership gating: `run.user_id == current_user` (or `submission.user_id`)

### Database
- **Driver**: `asyncpg` via `sqlalchemy[asyncio]`
- **Schema creation**: `Base.metadata.create_all` on startup (lifespan)
- **URL**: `postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mlperf` (from `DATABASE_URL` env var)

### Models & Constraints (enforced in SQLAlchemy + DB)
| Constraint | Applies to |
|---|---|
| `status IN (...)` — 9 valid values | Submission |
| `division IN ('standardized','serviced','rdi')` | Submission |
| `availability IN ('available','preview','rdi')` | Submission |
| `availability != 'preview' OR target_availability_date IS NOT NULL` | Submission |

### `expires_at` default
On Run CREATE: if `expires_at` is not supplied, default to `upload_time + 365 days` (i.e. `datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=365)`). No constraint linking `pinned` to `expires_at`.

---

## API Endpoints

### Runs (`/runs`)
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | `/runs` | required | Create run — `user_id` taken from `X-User-Id` header, not request body |
| GET | `/runs?user_id=` | optional | List runs (defaults to `X-User-Id`) |
| GET | `/runs/{id}` | none | Get run by ID |
| PATCH | `/runs/{id}/pin` | required | Pin (clears expires_at), gated by owner |
| PATCH | `/runs/{id}/unpin` | required | Unpin (sets expires_at = now+365d), gated by owner |
| DELETE | `/runs/{id}` | required | Delete, gated by owner |

### Submissions (`/submissions`)
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| POST | `/submissions` | none | Create submission |
| GET | `/submissions?user_id=` | optional | List (defaults to `X-User-Id`) |
| GET | `/submissions/{id}?include_runs=true` | none | Get submission + all run data |
| POST | `/submissions/{id}/withdraw` | required | Set withdrawn_at=now(), status=WITHDRAWN, gated by owner |
| PATCH | `/submissions/{id}` | required | Update timestamps, PR fields, status — gated by owner |

---

## Schemas (Pydantic v2)

- `RunCreate` — no `user_id` field (taken from header); no pinned/expires_at constraint; expires_at defaults to now+365d if omitted
- `RunOut` — full run response
- `SubmissionCreate` — validates division/availability enums, preview requires target date
- `SubmissionUpdate` — optional fields: status, all lifecycle timestamps, pr_url/pr_number, archive_uri
- `SubmissionOut` — full submission response
- `SubmissionWithRuns(SubmissionOut)` — adds `runs: List[RunOut]`

---

## Devcontainer

- **app service**: `mcr.microsoft.com/devcontainers/python:3.12`, volume-mounts workspace, waits for `db` healthcheck
- **db service**: `postgres:16`, healthcheck via `pg_isready`, port 5432 exposed
- `postStartCommand`: `pip install -r /workspace/requirements.txt`
- Run server: `uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload`

---

## Verification

1. Open devcontainer in VS Code (or `docker compose up` from `.devcontainer/`)
2. Run `uvicorn app.main:app --reload` — tables created on startup
3. `GET /health` → `{"status": "ok"}`
4. `POST /runs` with JSON body → 201 + run object with UUID
5. `GET /runs?user_id=alice` → list of alice's runs
6. `PATCH /runs/{id}/pin` with `X-User-Id: alice` → pinned=true, expires_at=null
7. `POST /submissions` → 201 + submission
8. `GET /submissions/{id}` → submission with nested runs array
9. `POST /submissions/{id}/withdraw` → status=WITHDRAWN
10. FastAPI auto-docs at `http://localhost:8000/docs`
```

import os
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, async_sessionmaker, AsyncSession
from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase

DATABASE_URL = os.getenv(
    "DATABASE_URL",
    "postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@db:5432/mlperf",
)

engine = create_async_engine(DATABASE_URL, echo=True)
AsyncSessionLocal = async_sessionmaker(engine, expire_on_commit=False)


class Base(DeclarativeBase):
    pass


async def get_db() -> AsyncSession:
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
        yield session

    peer_review_started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(TIMESTAMP, nullable=True)
    objection_resolution_started_at: Mapped[datetime | None] = mapped_column(TIMESTAMP, nullable=True)

        CheckConstraint("division IN ('standardized','serviced','rdi')", name="valid_division"),
        CheckConstraint("availability IN ('available','preview','rdi')", name="valid_availability"),
        CheckConstraint(
            "availability != 'preview' OR target_availability_date IS NOT NULL",
            name="preview_requires_target",
        ),
    )


class Submitter(Base):

from __future__ import annotations
from datetime import date, datetime
from typing import Any, Optional, List
from uuid import UUID
from pydantic import BaseModel, model_validator

VALID_STATUSES = {
    "COMPLIANCE_CHECKING", "PEER_REVIEW_PENDING", "CONFIDENTIAL_REVIEW",
    "FINALIZED", "PUBLISHED", "WITHDRAWN", "WITHDRAWN_INCOMPLETE",
    "EXPIRED", "SUPERSEDED",
}
VALID_DIVISIONS = {"standardized", "serviced", "rdi"}
VALID_AVAILABILITIES = {"available", "preview", "rdi"}


# ── Runs ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class RunCreate(BaseModel):
    started_at: datetime
    finished_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    pinned: bool = False
    system_info: dict[str, Any]
    config: dict[str, Any]
    result_summary: dict[str, Any]
    archive_uri: str


class RunOut(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    user_id: str
    started_at: datetime
    finished_at: datetime
    expires_at: Optional[datetime]
    pinned: bool
    system_info: dict[str, Any]
    config: dict[str, Any]
    result_summary: dict[str, Any]
    archive_uri: str

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


# ── Submissions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class SubmissionCreate(BaseModel):
    benchmark_version: int = 1
    division: str
    availability: str
    early_publish: bool = False
    publication_cycle: Optional[str] = None
    target_availability_date: Optional[date] = None
    run_ids: List[UUID]

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_fields(self) -> SubmissionCreate:
        if self.division not in VALID_DIVISIONS:
            raise ValueError(f"division must be one of {VALID_DIVISIONS}")
        if self.availability not in VALID_AVAILABILITIES:
            raise ValueError(f"availability must be one of {VALID_AVAILABILITIES}")
        if self.availability == "preview" and self.target_availability_date is None:
            raise ValueError("target_availability_date required when availability is 'preview'")
        return self


class SubmissionUpdate(BaseModel):
    status: Optional[str] = None
    availability_qualified_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    compliance_passed_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    first_published_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    peer_review_started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    objection_resolution_started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    finalized_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    pr_url: Optional[str] = None
    pr_number: Optional[int] = None
    archive_uri: Optional[str] = None
    publication_cycle: Optional[str] = None
    target_availability_date: Optional[date] = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_status(self) -> SubmissionUpdate:
        if self.status is not None and self.status not in VALID_STATUSES:
            raise ValueError(f"status must be one of {VALID_STATUSES}")
        return self


class SubmissionOut(BaseModel):
    id: UUID
    user_id: str
    created_at: datetime
    status: str
    benchmark_version: int
    division: str
    availability: str
    early_publish: bool
    publication_cycle: Optional[str]
    target_availability_date: Optional[date]
    availability_qualified_at: Optional[datetime]
    compliance_passed_at: Optional[datetime]
    first_published_at: Optional[datetime]
    peer_review_started_at: Optional[datetime]
    objection_resolution_started_at: Optional[datetime]
    finalized_at: Optional[datetime]
    withdrawn_at: Optional[datetime]
    run_ids: List[UUID]
    archive_uri: Optional[str]
    pr_url: Optional[str]
    pr_number: Optional[int]

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


class SubmissionWithRuns(SubmissionOut):
    runs: List[RunOut] = []

```

```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Optional
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from app.auth import require_user, optional_user
from app.database import get_db
from app.models import Run
from app.schemas import RunCreate, RunOut

router = APIRouter(prefix="/runs", tags=["runs"])


@router.post("", response_model=RunOut, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_run(
    body: RunCreate,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: str = Depends(require_user),
):
    expires_at = body.expires_at
    if expires_at is None:
        expires_at = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=365)

    run = Run(
        user_id=current_user,
        started_at=body.started_at,
        finished_at=body.finished_at,
        expires_at=expires_at,
        pinned=body.pinned,
        system_info=body.system_info,
        config=body.config,
        result_summary=body.result_summary,
        archive_uri=body.archive_uri,
    )
    db.add(run)
    await db.commit()
    await db.refresh(run)
    return run


@router.get("", response_model=list[RunOut])
async def list_runs(
    user_id: Optional[str] = Query(None),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: Optional[str] = Depends(optional_user),
):
    target = user_id or current_user
    if not target:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Provide user_id query param or X-User-Id header",
        )
    result = await db.execute(select(Run).where(Run.user_id == target))
    return result.scalars().all()


@router.get("/{run_id}", response_model=RunOut)
async def get_run(run_id: UUID, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    run = await db.get(Run, run_id)
    if not run:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Run not found")
    return run


@router.patch("/{run_id}/pin", response_model=RunOut)
async def pin_run(
    run_id: UUID,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: str = Depends(require_user),
):
    run = await db.get(Run, run_id)
    if not run:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Run not found")
    if run.user_id != current_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Not authorized")
    run.pinned = True
    run.expires_at = None
    await db.commit()
    await db.refresh(run)
    return run


@router.patch("/{run_id}/unpin", response_model=RunOut)
async def unpin_run(
    run_id: UUID,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: str = Depends(require_user),
):
    run = await db.get(Run, run_id)
    if not run:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Run not found")
    if run.user_id != current_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Not authorized")
    run.pinned = False
    run.expires_at = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=365)
    await db.commit()
    await db.refresh(run)
    return run


@router.delete("/{run_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def delete_run(
    run_id: UUID,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: str = Depends(require_user),
):
    run = await db.get(Run, run_id)
    if not run:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Run not found")
    if run.user_id != current_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Not authorized")
    await db.delete(run)
    await db.commit()

```

```python
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional
from uuid import UUID

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from app.auth import require_user, optional_user
from app.database import get_db
from app.models import Run, Submission
from app.schemas import RunOut, SubmissionCreate, SubmissionOut, SubmissionUpdate, SubmissionWithRuns

router = APIRouter(prefix="/submissions", tags=["submissions"])


@router.post("", response_model=SubmissionOut, status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_submission(
    body: SubmissionCreate,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: str = Depends(require_user),
):
    submission = Submission(
        user_id=current_user,
        benchmark_version=body.benchmark_version,
        division=body.division,
        availability=body.availability,
        early_publish=body.early_publish,
        publication_cycle=body.publication_cycle,
        target_availability_date=body.target_availability_date,
        run_ids=body.run_ids,
    )
    db.add(submission)
    await db.commit()
    await db.refresh(submission)
    return submission


@router.get("", response_model=list[SubmissionOut])
async def list_submissions(
    user_id: Optional[str] = Query(None),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: Optional[str] = Depends(optional_user),
):
    target = user_id or current_user
    if not target:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            detail="Provide user_id query param or X-User-Id header",
        )
    result = await db.execute(select(Submission).where(Submission.user_id == target))
    return result.scalars().all()


@router.get("/{submission_id}", response_model=SubmissionWithRuns)
async def get_submission(
    submission_id: UUID,
    include_runs: bool = Query(True),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
):
    submission = await db.get(Submission, submission_id)
    if not submission:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Submission not found")

    runs: list[RunOut] = []
    if include_runs and submission.run_ids:
        result = await db.execute(select(Run).where(Run.id.in_(submission.run_ids)))
        runs = [RunOut.model_validate(r) for r in result.scalars().all()]

    out = SubmissionWithRuns.model_validate(submission)
    out.runs = runs
    return out


@router.post("/{submission_id}/withdraw", response_model=SubmissionOut)
async def withdraw_submission(
    submission_id: UUID,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: str = Depends(require_user),
):
    submission = await db.get(Submission, submission_id)
    if not submission:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Submission not found")
    if submission.user_id != current_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Not authorized")
    if submission.withdrawn_at is not None:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT, detail="Submission already withdrawn")

    submission.withdrawn_at = datetime.utcnow()
    submission.status = "WITHDRAWN"
    await db.commit()
    await db.refresh(submission)
    return submission


@router.patch("/{submission_id}", response_model=SubmissionOut)
async def update_submission(
    submission_id: UUID,
    body: SubmissionUpdate,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    current_user: str = Depends(require_user),
):
    submission = await db.get(Submission, submission_id)
    if not submission:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Submission not found")
    if submission.user_id != current_user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Not authorized")

    for field, value in body.model_dump(exclude_none=True).items():
        setattr(submission, field, value)

    await db.commit()
    await db.refresh(submission)
    return submission

```

```python
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from fastapi import FastAPI
from app.database import engine, Base
import app.models  # noqa: F401 — registers models with Base
from app.routers import runs, submissions


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)
    yield


app = FastAPI(
    title="MLPerf Submission API",
    version="0.1.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

app.include_router(runs.router)
app.include_router(submissions.router)


@app.get("/health", tags=["meta"])
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok"}

```

```sql
-- Reference schema — tables are created automatically by SQLAlchemy on startup.
-- Run this manually if you need to seed a fresh database outside the app.

CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto";

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS runs (
    id              UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id         TEXT        NOT NULL,
    started_at      TIMESTAMP   NOT NULL,
    finished_at     TIMESTAMP   NOT NULL,
    expires_at      TIMESTAMP,
    pinned          BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    system_info     JSONB       NOT NULL,
    config          JSONB       NOT NULL,
    result_summary  JSONB       NOT NULL,
    archive_uri     TEXT        NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_runs_user_id ON runs (user_id);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS submissions (
    id                                  UUID        PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id                             TEXT        NOT NULL,
    created_at                          TIMESTAMP   NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    status                              TEXT        NOT NULL DEFAULT 'COMPLIANCE_CHECKING',
    benchmark_version                   INT         NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    division                            TEXT        NOT NULL,
    availability                        TEXT        NOT NULL,
    early_publish                       BOOLEAN     NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
    publication_cycle                   TEXT,
    target_availability_date            DATE,
    availability_qualified_at           TIMESTAMP,
    compliance_passed_at                TIMESTAMP,
    first_published_at                  TIMESTAMP,
    peer_review_started_at              TIMESTAMP,
    objection_resolution_started_at     TIMESTAMP,
    finalized_at                        TIMESTAMP,
    withdrawn_at                        TIMESTAMP,
    run_ids                             UUID[]      NOT NULL,
    archive_uri                         TEXT,
    pr_url                              TEXT,
    pr_number                           INT,

    CONSTRAINT valid_status CHECK (status IN (
        'COMPLIANCE_CHECKING', 'PEER_REVIEW_PENDING', 'CONFIDENTIAL_REVIEW',
        'FINALIZED', 'PUBLISHED', 'WITHDRAWN', 'WITHDRAWN_INCOMPLETE',
        'EXPIRED', 'SUPERSEDED'
    )),
    CONSTRAINT valid_division     CHECK (division     IN ('standardized', 'serviced', 'rdi')),
    CONSTRAINT valid_availability CHECK (availability IN ('available', 'preview', 'rdi')),
    CONSTRAINT preview_requires_target CHECK (
        availability != 'preview' OR target_availability_date IS NOT NULL
    )
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS submitters (
    id           UUID  PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    user_id      TEXT  NOT NULL,
    organization TEXT  NOT NULL
);

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 12:14:20Z)**_

All files pass syntax check. Here's what was built:

---

**Project structure:**
```
.devcontainer/
  devcontainer.json     — VS Code devcontainer (python:3.12 + postgres:16)
  docker-compose.yml    — app + db services, healthcheck, port forwarding
app/
  main.py               — FastAPI app, table creation on startup via lifespan
  database.py           — async SQLAlchemy engine + session factory
  models.py             — Run, Submission, Submitter ORM models with DB constraints
  schemas.py            — Pydantic v2 request/response models + enum validators
  auth.py               — X-User-Id header extraction (PRISM placeholder)
  routers/
    runs.py             — POST /runs, GET /runs, GET /runs/{id}, PATCH pin/unpin, DELETE
    submissions.py      — POST, GET list, GET /{id} w/ runs, POST withdraw, PATCH
migrations/
  001_init.sql          — reference SQL (app auto-creates tables on startup)
requirements.txt
```

**Key behaviours:**
- `expires_at` defaults to `now + 365 days` on run create if omitted
- `user_id` is set server-side from `X-User-Id` header on create (not trusted from body)
- Pin/unpin/delete/withdraw/update are all gated to the owning user
- `GET /submissions/{id}?include_runs=true` (default) fetches all run records inline

**To start inside the devcontainer:**
```bash
uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload
```
Docs at `http://localhost:8000/docs`.

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, async_sessionmaker

from app.main import app
from app.database import Base, get_db

TEST_DB_URL = "postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/mlperf_test"

test_engine = create_async_engine(TEST_DB_URL, echo=False)
TestSessionLocal = async_sessionmaker(test_engine, expire_on_commit=False)


@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
async def reset_db():
    async with test_engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)
    yield
    async with test_engine.begin() as conn:
        for table in reversed(Base.metadata.sorted_tables):
            await conn.execute(table.delete())


@pytest.fixture
async def client():
    async def override_get_db():
        async with TestSessionLocal() as session:
            yield session

    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = override_get_db
    async with AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url="http://test") as c:
        yield c
    app.dependency_overrides.clear()


# ── Shared payloads ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

}

}

```python
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient

from tests.conftest import RUN_PAYLOAD

USER_ALICE = {"X-User-Id": "alice"}
USER_BOB = {"X-User-Id": "bob"}


# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def create_run(client: AsyncClient, headers=USER_ALICE, **overrides) -> dict:
    payload = {**RUN_PAYLOAD, **overrides}
    res = await client.post("/runs", headers=headers, json=payload)
    assert res.status_code == 201, res.text
    return res.json()


# ── Health ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def test_health(client: AsyncClient):
    res = await client.get("/health")
    assert res.status_code == 200
    assert res.json() == {"status": "ok"}


# ── CREATE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestCreateRun:
    async def test_happy_path(self, client: AsyncClient):
        data = await create_run(client)
        assert uuid.UUID(data["id"])
        assert data["user_id"] == "alice"
        assert data["pinned"] is False
        assert data["archive_uri"] == RUN_PAYLOAD["archive_uri"]

    async def test_user_id_from_header_not_body(self, client: AsyncClient):
        data = await create_run(client, headers={"X-User-Id": "charlie"})
        assert data["user_id"] == "charlie"

    async def test_missing_auth_header(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.post("/runs", json=RUN_PAYLOAD)
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_default_expires_at_is_365_days(self, client: AsyncClient):
        data = await create_run(client)
        expires = datetime.fromisoformat(data["expires_at"])
        expected = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=365)
        assert abs((expires - expected).total_seconds()) < 60

    async def test_explicit_expires_at_is_respected(self, client: AsyncClient):
        explicit = "2030-06-01T00:00:00"
        data = await create_run(client, expires_at=explicit)
        assert data["expires_at"].startswith("2030-06-01")

    async def test_pinned_run_sets_no_expires_at(self, client: AsyncClient):
        data = await create_run(client, pinned=True)
        assert data["pinned"] is True
        # expires_at is still set by default (no constraint linking pin to expiry)
        # but user may have passed None explicitly
        assert data["expires_at"] is not None  # default still applied

    async def test_pinned_run_with_null_expires_at(self, client: AsyncClient):
        payload = {**RUN_PAYLOAD, "pinned": True, "expires_at": None}
        # explicitly setting expires_at=None while pinned should still default
        res = await client.post("/runs", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
        assert res.status_code == 201
        assert res.json()["pinned"] is True

    async def test_missing_required_fields(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.post("/runs", headers=USER_ALICE, json={"started_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00"})
        assert res.status_code == 422


# ── LIST ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestListRuns:
    async def test_list_by_header(self, client: AsyncClient):
        await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.get("/runs", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert len(res.json()) == 2

    async def test_list_by_query_param(self, client: AsyncClient):
        await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.get("/runs", params={"user_id": "alice"})
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert len(res.json()) == 1

    async def test_query_param_takes_priority_over_header(self, client: AsyncClient):
        await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        await create_run(client, headers=USER_BOB)
        res = await client.get("/runs", headers=USER_ALICE, params={"user_id": "bob"})
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert all(r["user_id"] == "bob" for r in res.json())

    async def test_no_context_returns_400(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.get("/runs")
        assert res.status_code == 400

    async def test_empty_list_for_unknown_user(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.get("/runs", headers={"X-User-Id": "nobody"})
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert res.json() == []

    async def test_isolation_between_users(self, client: AsyncClient):
        await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        await create_run(client, headers=USER_BOB)
        alice_runs = (await client.get("/runs", headers=USER_ALICE)).json()
        bob_runs = (await client.get("/runs", headers=USER_BOB)).json()
        assert len(alice_runs) == 1
        assert len(bob_runs) == 1
        assert alice_runs[0]["user_id"] == "alice"
        assert bob_runs[0]["user_id"] == "bob"


# ── GET ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestGetRun:
    async def test_happy_path(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client)
        res = await client.get(f"/runs/{created['id']}")
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert res.json()["id"] == created["id"]

    async def test_not_found(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.get(f"/runs/{uuid.uuid4()}")
        assert res.status_code == 404

    async def test_returns_full_payload(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client)
        data = (await client.get(f"/runs/{created['id']}")).json()
        assert data["system_info"] == RUN_PAYLOAD["system_info"]
        assert data["config"] == RUN_PAYLOAD["config"]
        assert data["result_summary"] == RUN_PAYLOAD["result_summary"]


# ── PIN ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestPinRun:
    async def test_pin_sets_flag_and_clears_expiry(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client)
        assert created["expires_at"] is not None

        res = await client.patch(f"/runs/{created['id']}/pin", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 200
        data = res.json()
        assert data["pinned"] is True
        assert data["expires_at"] is None

    async def test_pin_nonowner_forbidden(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.patch(f"/runs/{created['id']}/pin", headers=USER_BOB)
        assert res.status_code == 403

    async def test_pin_missing_auth(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client)
        res = await client.patch(f"/runs/{created['id']}/pin")
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_pin_not_found(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.patch(f"/runs/{uuid.uuid4()}/pin", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 404


# ── UNPIN ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestUnpinRun:
    async def test_unpin_restores_expiry(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client)
        run_id = created["id"]
        await client.patch(f"/runs/{run_id}/pin", headers=USER_ALICE)

        res = await client.patch(f"/runs/{run_id}/unpin", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 200
        data = res.json()
        assert data["pinned"] is False
        assert data["expires_at"] is not None
        expires = datetime.fromisoformat(data["expires_at"])
        assert abs((expires - (datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=365))).total_seconds()) < 60

    async def test_unpin_nonowner_forbidden(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.patch(f"/runs/{created['id']}/unpin", headers=USER_BOB)
        assert res.status_code == 403


# ── DELETE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestDeleteRun:
    async def test_happy_path(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client)
        run_id = created["id"]

        res = await client.delete(f"/runs/{run_id}", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 204

        res = await client.get(f"/runs/{run_id}")
        assert res.status_code == 404

    async def test_delete_nonowner_forbidden(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.delete(f"/runs/{created['id']}", headers=USER_BOB)
        assert res.status_code == 403

    async def test_delete_not_found(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.delete(f"/runs/{uuid.uuid4()}", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 404

    async def test_delete_missing_auth(self, client: AsyncClient):
        created = await create_run(client)
        res = await client.delete(f"/runs/{created['id']}")
        assert res.status_code == 422

```python
import uuid

import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient

from tests.conftest import RUN_PAYLOAD, SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD

USER_ALICE = {"X-User-Id": "alice"}
USER_BOB = {"X-User-Id": "bob"}


# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

async def create_run(client: AsyncClient, headers=USER_ALICE) -> str:
    res = await client.post("/runs", headers=headers, json=RUN_PAYLOAD)
    assert res.status_code == 201, res.text
    return res.json()["id"]


async def create_submission(client: AsyncClient, headers=USER_ALICE, **overrides) -> dict:
    run_id = await create_run(client, headers=headers)
    payload = {**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run_id], **overrides}
    res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=headers, json=payload)
    assert res.status_code == 201, res.text
    return res.json()


# ── CREATE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestCreateSubmission:
    async def test_happy_path(self, client: AsyncClient):
        data = await create_submission(client)
        assert uuid.UUID(data["id"])
        assert data["user_id"] == "alice"
        assert data["status"] == "COMPLIANCE_CHECKING"
        assert data["division"] == "standardized"
        assert data["availability"] == "available"
        assert len(data["run_ids"]) == 1

    async def test_missing_auth(self, client: AsyncClient):
        run_id = await create_run(client)
        res = await client.post("/submissions", json={**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run_id]})
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_invalid_division(self, client: AsyncClient):
        run_id = await create_run(client)
        payload = {**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run_id], "division": "invalid"}
        res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_invalid_availability(self, client: AsyncClient):
        run_id = await create_run(client)
        payload = {**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run_id], "availability": "bad"}
        res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_preview_requires_target_date(self, client: AsyncClient):
        run_id = await create_run(client)
        payload = {**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run_id], "availability": "preview"}
        res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_preview_with_target_date_ok(self, client: AsyncClient):
        run_id = await create_run(client)
        payload = {
            **SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD,
            "run_ids": [run_id],
            "availability": "preview",
            "target_availability_date": "2025-06-01",
        }
        res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
        assert res.status_code == 201
        assert res.json()["target_availability_date"] == "2025-06-01"

    async def test_multiple_run_ids(self, client: AsyncClient):
        run1 = await create_run(client)
        run2 = await create_run(client)
        payload = {**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run1, run2]}
        res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
        assert res.status_code == 201
        assert len(res.json()["run_ids"]) == 2

    async def test_all_divisions(self, client: AsyncClient):
        for division in ("standardized", "serviced", "rdi"):
            run_id = await create_run(client)
            payload = {**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run_id], "division": division}
            res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
            assert res.status_code == 201, f"division={division} failed: {res.text}"

    async def test_all_availabilities(self, client: AsyncClient):
        for avail in ("available", "rdi"):
            run_id = await create_run(client)
            payload = {**SUBMISSION_PAYLOAD, "run_ids": [run_id], "availability": avail}
            res = await client.post("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE, json=payload)
            assert res.status_code == 201, f"availability={avail} failed: {res.text}"


# ── LIST ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestListSubmissions:
    async def test_list_by_header(self, client: AsyncClient):
        await create_submission(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        await create_submission(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.get("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert len(res.json()) == 2

    async def test_list_by_query_param(self, client: AsyncClient):
        await create_submission(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.get("/submissions", params={"user_id": "alice"})
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert len(res.json()) == 1

    async def test_no_context_returns_400(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.get("/submissions")
        assert res.status_code == 400

    async def test_isolation_between_users(self, client: AsyncClient):
        await create_submission(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        await create_submission(client, headers=USER_BOB)
        alice = (await client.get("/submissions", headers=USER_ALICE)).json()
        bob = (await client.get("/submissions", headers=USER_BOB)).json()
        assert len(alice) == 1 and alice[0]["user_id"] == "alice"
        assert len(bob) == 1 and bob[0]["user_id"] == "bob"


# ── GET (with runs) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestGetSubmission:
    async def test_returns_submission_with_runs(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        res = await client.get(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}")
        assert res.status_code == 200
        data = res.json()
        assert data["id"] == sub["id"]
        assert len(data["runs"]) == 1
        assert data["runs"][0]["archive_uri"] == RUN_PAYLOAD["archive_uri"]

    async def test_include_runs_false_returns_empty_runs(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        res = await client.get(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}", params={"include_runs": False})
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert res.json()["runs"] == []

    async def test_not_found(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.get(f"/submissions/{uuid.uuid4()}")
        assert res.status_code == 404

    async def test_lifecycle_fields_initially_null(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        data = (await client.get(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}")).json()
        for field in ("compliance_passed_at", "first_published_at", "withdrawn_at",
                      "peer_review_started_at", "finalized_at"):
            assert data[field] is None, f"{field} should be null initially"


# ── WITHDRAW ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestWithdrawSubmission:
    async def test_happy_path(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        res = await client.post(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}/withdraw", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 200
        data = res.json()
        assert data["status"] == "WITHDRAWN"
        assert data["withdrawn_at"] is not None

    async def test_already_withdrawn_returns_409(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        await client.post(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}/withdraw", headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.post(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}/withdraw", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 409

    async def test_nonowner_forbidden(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.post(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}/withdraw", headers=USER_BOB)
        assert res.status_code == 403

    async def test_missing_auth(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        res = await client.post(f"/submissions/{sub['id']}/withdraw")
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_not_found(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.post(f"/submissions/{uuid.uuid4()}/withdraw", headers=USER_ALICE)
        assert res.status_code == 404


# ── UPDATE ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestUpdateSubmission:
    async def test_update_status(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        res = await client.patch(
            f"/submissions/{sub['id']}",
            headers=USER_ALICE,
            json={"status": "PEER_REVIEW_PENDING"},
        )
        assert res.status_code == 200
        assert res.json()["status"] == "PEER_REVIEW_PENDING"

    async def test_update_pr_fields(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        res = await client.patch(
            f"/submissions/{sub['id']}",
            headers=USER_ALICE,
            json={"pr_url": "https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42", "pr_number": 42},
        )
        assert res.status_code == 200
        data = res.json()
        assert data["pr_url"] == "https://github.com/org/repo/pull/42"
        assert data["pr_number"] == 42

    async def test_update_lifecycle_timestamps(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        ts = "2024-06-01T12:00:00"
        res = await client.patch(
            f"/submissions/{sub['id']}",
            headers=USER_ALICE,
            json={"compliance_passed_at": ts, "peer_review_started_at": ts},
        )
        assert res.status_code == 200
        data = res.json()
        assert data["compliance_passed_at"] is not None
        assert data["peer_review_started_at"] is not None

    async def test_invalid_status_rejected(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client)
        res = await client.patch(
            f"/submissions/{sub['id']}",
            headers=USER_ALICE,
            json={"status": "INVALID_STATUS"},
        )
        assert res.status_code == 422

    async def test_nonowner_forbidden(self, client: AsyncClient):
        sub = await create_submission(client, headers=USER_ALICE)
        res = await client.patch(
            f"/submissions/{sub['id']}",
            headers=USER_BOB,
            json={"status": "PEER_REVIEW_PENDING"},
        )
        assert res.status_code == 403

    async def test_not_found(self, client: AsyncClient):
        res = await client.patch(
            f"/submissions/{uuid.uuid4()}",
            headers=USER_ALICE,
            json={"status": "PEER_REVIEW_PENDING"},
        )
        assert res.status_code == 404

    async def test_all_valid_statuses(self, client: AsyncClient):
        valid = [
            "COMPLIANCE_CHECKING", "PEER_REVIEW_PENDING", "CONFIDENTIAL_REVIEW",
            "FINALIZED", "PUBLISHED", "WITHDRAWN", "WITHDRAWN_INCOMPLETE",
            "EXPIRED", "SUPERSEDED",
        ]
        for s in valid:
            sub = await create_submission(client)
            res = await client.patch(
                f"/submissions/{sub['id']}",
                headers=USER_ALICE,
                json={"status": s},
            )
            assert res.status_code == 200, f"status={s} rejected: {res.text}"

34	    await db.commit()
35	    await db.refresh(submission)

39	@router.get("", response_model=list[SubmissionOut])
40	async def list_submissions(
41	    user_id: Optional[str] = Query(None),
42	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
43	    current_user: Optional[str] = Depends(optional_user),
44	):

55	@router.get("/{submission_id}", response_model=SubmissionWithRuns)
56	async def get_submission(
57	    submission_id: UUID,
58	    include_runs: bool = Query(True),
59	    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
60	):
61	    submission = await db.get(Submission, submission_id)
62	    if not submission:
63	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND, detail="Submission not found")
64	
65	    runs: list[RunOut] = []
66	    if include_runs and submission.run_ids:

68	        runs = [RunOut.model_validate(r) for r in result.scalars().all()]

84	    if submission.user_id != current_user:
85	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Not authorized")
86	    if submission.withdrawn_at is not None:
87	        raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT, detail="Submission already withdrawn")
88	
89	    submission.withdrawn_at = datetime.utcnow()

109	    for field, value in body.model_dump(exclude_none=True).items():

**58/58 tests passing, 100% coverage.** Here's the summary:

---

**Tests (58 total):**
- `test_health` — smoke test
- `TestCreateRun` (8) — happy path, header-based user_id, missing auth, expires_at default/explicit/with-pin, missing fields
- `TestListRuns` (6) — by header, by query param, param wins over header, 400 with no context, empty list, user isolation
- `TestGetRun` (3) — happy path, 404, full payload round-trip
- `TestPinRun` (4) — pin clears expiry, non-owner 403, missing auth 422, 404
- `TestUnpinRun` (3) — restores expiry to +365d, non-owner 403, 404
- `TestDeleteRun` (4) — deletes then 404s, non-owner 403, 404, missing auth 422
- `TestCreateSubmission` (9) — happy path, missing auth, bad division/availability, preview without target, preview with target, multiple run_ids, all divisions, both non-preview availabilities
- `TestListSubmissions` (4) — by header, by query param, 400 no context, user isolation
- `TestGetSubmission` (4) — with runs, without runs, 404, lifecycle nulls
- `TestWithdrawSubmission` (5) — happy path, idempotency 409, non-owner 403, missing auth 422, 404
- `TestUpdateSubmission` (7) — status, PR fields, lifecycle timestamps, invalid status 422, non-owner 403, 404, all 9 valid statuses

**Coverage:** 100% of meaningful app code (`get_db` and lifespan body excluded via `pragma: no cover` — both are infrastructure-only, only exercised when the real server starts).

**`openapi.json`:** 1,431-line OpenAPI 3.1 spec generated at project root, covering all 12 endpoints with full request/response schemas.

How do I run the tests

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 12:56:07Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 12:56:11Z)**_

From the `/home/arav/test2` directory:

**Prerequisites — postgres test container must be running:**
```bash
docker start mlperf-test-pg
# or start fresh:
docker run -d --name mlperf-test-pg \
  -e POSTGRES_USER=postgres \
  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres \
  -e POSTGRES_DB=mlperf_test \
  -p 5433:5432 postgres:16
```

**Install deps (first time only):**
```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt pytest pytest-asyncio httpx pytest-cov
```

**Run tests:**
```bash
# All tests
python -m pytest tests/ -v

# With coverage
python -m pytest tests/ --cov=app --cov-report=term-missing

# Single file
python -m pytest tests/test_runs.py -v

# Single test
python -m pytest tests/test_runs.py::TestPinRun::test_pin_sets_flag_and_clears_expiry -v
```

**Generate the OpenAPI schema:**
```bash
python generate_openapi.py
```

_**User (2026-05-04 12:56:20Z)**_

How do I run the dev container

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 12:56:22Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 12:56:25Z)**_

**Option 1 — VS Code (recommended):**
1. Install the [Dev Containers extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-containers)
2. Open the `test2` folder in VS Code
3. When prompted "Reopen in Container", click it — or open the Command Palette (`Ctrl+Shift+P`) → **Dev Containers: Reopen in Container**

VS Code will build the containers, install deps, and drop you into a shell inside the `app` container with the database already running.

**Option 2 — Docker Compose directly (no VS Code):**
```bash
cd .devcontainer
docker compose up -d
docker compose exec app bash
```

**Once inside the container, start the API:**
```bash
uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload
```

API is then at `http://localhost:8000` and docs at `http://localhost:8000/docs`.

**To stop:**
```bash
cd .devcontainer
docker compose down
```

<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
# MLPerf Submission API

Version: `0.1.0`

## Authentication

Write operations require an `X-User-Id` header. This is a placeholder for PRISM authentication — the value becomes the `user_id` on any created resource and is checked for ownership on mutations.

---

## Runs

### Create Run

`POST /runs`

Creates a new benchmark run. `user_id` is taken from the `X-User-Id` header. If `expires_at` is omitted it defaults to **upload time + 365 days**.

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | Yes | PRISM user ID |

**Request body** `application/json`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `started_at` | datetime | Yes | — | Benchmark start time |
| `finished_at` | datetime | Yes | — | Benchmark end time |
| `expires_at` | datetime | No | now + 365 days | Expiry timestamp |
| `pinned` | boolean | No | `false` | Pin the run |
| `system_info` | object | Yes | — | Hardware/software description |
| `config` | object | Yes | — | Benchmark config (converted from YAML) |
| `result_summary` | object | Yes | — | Top-level result metrics |
| `archive_uri` | string | Yes | — | Blob storage URI for the full archive |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `201` | Run created — returns `RunOut` |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### List Runs

`GET /runs`

Returns all runs for a user. Provide the target via `user_id` query param or `X-User-Id` header (query param takes priority). Returns `400` if neither is provided.

**Query parameters**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `user_id` | No | Filter by this user ID |

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | No | Fallback user ID if `user_id` param is absent |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | Array of `RunOut` |
| `400` | No user context provided |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### Get Run

`GET /runs/{run_id}`

Returns a single run by ID.

**Path parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `run_id` | uuid | Run ID |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | `RunOut` |
| `404` | Run not found |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### Pin Run

`PATCH /runs/{run_id}/pin`

Pins the run and clears `expires_at`. Gated to the owning user.

**Path parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `run_id` | uuid | Run ID |

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | Yes | Must match `user_id` on the run |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | Updated `RunOut` — `pinned: true`, `expires_at: null` |
| `403` | Not the run owner |
| `404` | Run not found |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### Unpin Run

`PATCH /runs/{run_id}/unpin`

Unpins the run and resets `expires_at` to now + 365 days. Gated to the owning user.

**Path parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `run_id` | uuid | Run ID |

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | Yes | Must match `user_id` on the run |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | Updated `RunOut` — `pinned: false`, `expires_at` set |
| `403` | Not the run owner |
| `404` | Run not found |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### Delete Run

`DELETE /runs/{run_id}`

Permanently deletes a run. Gated to the owning user.

**Path parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `run_id` | uuid | Run ID |

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | Yes | Must match `user_id` on the run |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `204` | Deleted |
| `403` | Not the run owner |
| `404` | Run not found |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

## Submissions

### Create Submission

`POST /submissions`

Creates a new submission. `user_id` is taken from the `X-User-Id` header. `division` and `availability` must be valid enum values. `target_availability_date` is required when `availability` is `preview`.

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | Yes | PRISM user ID |

**Request body** `application/json`

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `division` | string | Yes | — | `standardized` \| `serviced` \| `rdi` |
| `availability` | string | Yes | — | `available` \| `preview` \| `rdi` |
| `run_ids` | uuid[] | Yes | — | IDs of runs included in this submission |
| `benchmark_version` | integer | No | `1` | Benchmark version |
| `early_publish` | boolean | No | `false` | Flag for early publication |
| `publication_cycle` | string | No | `null` | Cycle identifier (`YYYY-MM-CN`) |
| `target_availability_date` | date | No* | `null` | *Required when `availability` is `preview` |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `201` | Submission created — returns `SubmissionOut` |
| `422` | Validation error (includes invalid enum values or missing target date for preview) |

---

### List Submissions

`GET /submissions`

Returns all submissions for a user. Provide the target via `user_id` query param or `X-User-Id` header. Returns `400` if neither is provided.

**Query parameters**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `user_id` | No | Filter by this user ID |

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | No | Fallback user ID if `user_id` param is absent |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | Array of `SubmissionOut` |
| `400` | No user context provided |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### Get Submission

`GET /submissions/{submission_id}`

Returns a submission and optionally embeds the full data for all associated runs.

**Path parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `submission_id` | uuid | Submission ID |

**Query parameters**

| Name | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `include_runs` | boolean | No | `true` | Embed full run objects in the response |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | `SubmissionWithRuns` (includes `runs: RunOut[]`) |
| `404` | Submission not found |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### Withdraw Submission

`POST /submissions/{submission_id}/withdraw`

Sets `withdrawn_at` to now and transitions `status` to `WITHDRAWN`. Idempotency: returns `409` if already withdrawn. Gated to the owning user.

**Path parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `submission_id` | uuid | Submission ID |

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | Yes | Must match `user_id` on the submission |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | Updated `SubmissionOut` — `status: WITHDRAWN`, `withdrawn_at` set |
| `403` | Not the submission owner |
| `404` | Submission not found |
| `409` | Already withdrawn |
| `422` | Validation error |

---

### Update Submission

`PATCH /submissions/{submission_id}`

Updates mutable fields: lifecycle timestamps, status, PR metadata, and archive URI. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated. Gated to the owning user.

**Path parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `submission_id` | uuid | Submission ID |

**Headers**

| Name | Required | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `X-User-Id` | Yes | Must match `user_id` on the submission |

**Request body** `application/json` — all fields optional

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `status` | string | One of the valid statuses (see below) |
| `availability_qualified_at` | datetime | — |
| `compliance_passed_at` | datetime | — |
| `first_published_at` | datetime | — |
| `peer_review_started_at` | datetime | — |
| `objection_resolution_started_at` | datetime | — |
| `finalized_at` | datetime | — |
| `publication_cycle` | string | `YYYY-MM-CN` |
| `target_availability_date` | date | — |
| `archive_uri` | string | Blob storage URI |
| `pr_url` | string | GitHub PR URL |
| `pr_number` | integer | GitHub PR number |

**Responses**

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `200` | Updated `SubmissionOut` |
| `403` | Not the submission owner |
| `404` | Submission not found |
| `422` | Validation error (e.g. invalid status value) |

---

## Meta

### Health

`GET /health`

**Responses**

| Status | Body |
|--------|------|
| `200` | `{"status": "ok"}` |

---

## Schemas

### RunOut

| Field | Type | Nullable |
|-------|------|----------|
| `id` | uuid | No |
| `user_id` | string | No |
| `started_at` | datetime | No |
| `finished_at` | datetime | No |
| `expires_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `pinned` | boolean | No |
| `system_info` | object | No |
| `config` | object | No |
| `result_summary` | object | No |
| `archive_uri` | string | No |

### SubmissionOut

| Field | Type | Nullable |
|-------|------|----------|
| `id` | uuid | No |
| `user_id` | string | No |
| `created_at` | datetime | No |
| `status` | string | No |
| `benchmark_version` | integer | No |
| `division` | string | No |
| `availability` | string | No |
| `early_publish` | boolean | No |
| `publication_cycle` | string | Yes |
| `target_availability_date` | date | Yes |
| `availability_qualified_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `compliance_passed_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `first_published_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `peer_review_started_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `objection_resolution_started_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `finalized_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `withdrawn_at` | datetime | Yes |
| `run_ids` | uuid[] | No |
| `archive_uri` | string | Yes |
| `pr_url` | string | Yes |
| `pr_number` | integer | Yes |

### SubmissionWithRuns

Extends `SubmissionOut` with:

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `runs` | `RunOut[]` | Full run objects for each ID in `run_ids`. Empty array if `include_runs=false`. |

### Valid Submission Statuses

| Status | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `COMPLIANCE_CHECKING` | Default on creation |
| `PEER_REVIEW_PENDING` | Awaiting peer review |
| `CONFIDENTIAL_REVIEW` | Under confidential review |
| `FINALIZED` | Review complete |
| `PUBLISHED` | Publicly published |
| `WITHDRAWN` | Withdrawn by submitter |
| `WITHDRAWN_INCOMPLETE` | Withdrawn before completion |
| `EXPIRED` | Past expiry |
| `SUPERSEDED` | Replaced by a newer submission |

```

_**User (2026-05-04 13:04:05Z)**_

Why doesn't the markdown have an example output and input?

---

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 13:04:14Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 13:04:15Z)**_

Because the OpenAPI spec itself has no `example` fields — `openapi-markdown` can only output what's in the spec. The Pydantic models don't define any examples, so none end up in `openapi.json`.

The fix is to add `json_schema_extra` to each Pydantic model in `app/schemas.py`, which Pydantic v2 uses to inject examples into the OpenAPI spec. Want me to add them?

---

_**User (2026-05-04 13:08:48Z)**_

Yep. Use real outputs, however, from the app.

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me create a final summary for you.

## Summary: GitHub Repository Contents - PR-Tests Branch

I've successfully retrieved the contents of the **anandhu-eng/endpoints** repository on the `pr-tests` branch. Here's a complete overview of the example run/submission data available:

### Repository Structure

The branch contains **22 test directories** organized by:
- **Model/Framework**: `llama-3.1-8b_vllm_perf`
- **Concurrency Levels**: 1, 4, and 16 concurrent requests
- **Variants**: main_trial1 vs pr_trial1 (main branch vs PR branch comparisons)
- **Multiple Git Commits**: Various commit hashes for tracking different versions

### File Types Available (in each test directory)

Each test directory contains these key files:

1. **config.yaml** - Benchmark configuration

# Full Contents of Configuration File

This is a benchmark configuration for testing Llama 3.1 8B on the CNN DailyMail dataset. Here's what it contains:

**Model Setup:**
The configuration specifies "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct" with temperature 0.0, streaming enabled, and a 128-token output limit.

**Dataset Configuration:**
Two dataset entries use the CNN DailyMail corpus with 2,000 samples each. The first measures accuracy via ROUGE scores comparing generated text against reference highlights. The second tracks performance metrics.

**Runtime Parameters:**
The benchmark runs for 10-60 minutes, processing all 2,000 samples with fixed random seeds for reproducibility.

**Load Testing:**
It uses a concurrency pattern with a single concurrent request, four worker processes, and ZMQ transport with 16MB buffers for communication.

**Metrics Collected:**
The system gathers throughput, latency, first-token-to-first-response time (TTFT), and time-per-output-token (TPOT) measurements.

**Endpoint Configuration:**
Tests target a local OpenAI-compatible API running at "http://localhost:9000" with a 6000-second timeout and CPU affinity enabled.
```

class SubmissionCreate(BaseModel):
    benchmark_version: int = 1

    target_availability_date: Optional[date] = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def validate_fields(self) -> SubmissionCreate:
        if self.division not in VALID_DIVISIONS:
            raise ValueError(f"division must be one of {VALID_DIVISIONS}")
        if self.availability not in VALID_AVAILABILITIES:
            raise ValueError(f"availability must be one of {VALID_AVAILABILITIES}")
        if self.availability == "preview" and self.target_availability_date is None:
            raise ValueError("target_availability_date required when availability is 'preview'")
        return self

Returns 201 “OK” upon successful response and 403 “Validation Error” if run data has any schema-related issues ( Note: this does not validate a run. Run validation should happen in the CLI. )
GET /runs
With a user-id, gets all run_ids a user has made.
For display purposes, we’ll also get concurrency, model, and finished_at + started_at timestamps. We’ll want these to make it easier for a user to know what a run_id means in terms of the runs they’ve started, as I imagine they’d want to make runs in parallel.
GET /runs/{run_id}
For a particular run_id, get all of the run details. 
Validates that the user-id supplied in the header corresponds to the run, but nothing else. 
DELETE /runs/{run_id}
After validating that the user id and run id match, deletes a run.
Returns OK if successful.
PATCH /runs/{run_id}/pin
For a given {run_id}, pin it. 
Validates that the user_id belongs to the run.
Returns OK if successful.
PATCH /runs/{run_id}/unpin
For a given {run_id}, pin it. 
Validates that the user_id belongs to the run.
Returns OK if successful.
 instead. I think it's a little too much atp

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-04 13:36:17Z)**_

Got it. Here's what I'm changing:
- `POST /runs` — `user_id` as query param (not header)
- `GET /runs` — lightweight `RunSummary` (id, model, concurrency, started_at, finished_at)
- `GET /runs/{run_id}` — full details, validates `X-User-Id` header matches run owner
- `DELETE` — returns `200 {"status": "deleted"}` instead of 204
- Pin/unpin unchanged in behaviour, 200 responses

One note: FastAPI returns `422` for schema validation, not `403` — I'll keep that since 403 means Forbidden.
